agent_configs.system_prompt: Be critical. Each reply should question, challenge, or disagree with something in the previous message -- never just agree and elaborate.

injection.text: A honeybee colony is a single organism spread across forty thousand bodies. Workers dance in figure eights on the comb, angling their waggle toward the sun to tell sisters exactly where the flowers bloom. When the water warms even slightly, the corals expel their algae and turn ghostly white, a stress response called bleaching that can kill an entire reef if the heat lingers for too many weeks. Mordants like alum bind the colour to the fibre so it survives washing, and the same dye bath can give a dozen shades depending on the metal in the pot and the mineral content of the water. Good olive oil is fruit juice with a short shelf life, not a pantry staple that lasts forever.

injections.text: A honeybee colony is a single organism spread across forty thousand bodies. Workers dance in figure eights on the comb, angling their waggle toward the sun to tell sisters exactly where the flowers bloom. When the water warms even slightly, the corals expel their algae and turn ghostly white, a stress response called bleaching that can kill an entire reef if the heat lingers for too many weeks. Mordants like alum bind the colour to the fibre so it survives washing, and the same dye bath can give a dozen shades depending on the metal in the pot and the mineral content of the water. Good olive oil is fruit juice with a short shelf life, not a pantry staple that lasts forever.

Coral reefs are built by tiny animals called polyps that secrete limestone skeletons over thousands of years. Each polyp hosts symbiotic algae that photosynthesize and give the reef its vivid colour. Sourdough baking rewards patience above all else. A wild-yeast starter is a living culture of flour and water that you feed daily until it doubles reliably, smelling of ripe apples and yogurt. Sourdough baking rewards patience above all else. A wild-yeast starter is a living culture of flour and water that you feed daily until it doubles reliably, smelling of ripe apples and yogurt. Chess spread along trade routes from India through Persia to Europe, and the pieces changed as they travelled. The counsellor beside the king became a queen, gaining the sweeping power that makes the modern game so sharp. A volcano is a pressure valve for a planet still cooling from its birth. Magma rises because it is lighter than the surrounding rock, gathering in chambers until gas bubbles force it upward through cracks. Glaciers are rivers of ice that flow downhill at the pace of fingernails growing, grinding mountains into powder and carving valleys shaped like the letter U. The blue in deep crevasses is ancient compressed air. A honeybee colony is a single organism spread across forty thousand bodies. Workers dance in figure eights on the comb, angling their waggle toward the sun to tell sisters exactly where the flowers bloom. Sourdough baking rewards patience above all else. A wild-yeast starter is a living culture of flour and water that you feed daily until it doubles reliably, smelling of ripe apples and yogurt. Above five thousand metres the air holds half the oxygen it does at sea level, and the body begins to consume its own muscle to survive. Climbers move slowly, breathing four times for every step. Good olive oil is fruit juice with a short shelf life, not a pantry staple that lasts forever. The olives must be pressed within hours of harvest, before the flesh oxidises and turns rancid and dull. The Arctic tern lives in perpetual summer, flying from pole to pole each year and covering more than seventy thousand kilometres. Over a lifetime it travels far enough to reach the moon and back three times. Good olive oil is fruit juice with a short shelf life, not a pantry staple that lasts forever. The olives must be pressed within hours of harvest, before the flesh oxidises and turns rancid and dull. Lighthouses once depended on a single obsessive invention, the Fresnel lens, which folds glass into concentric rings that gather a small flame into a beam visible for thirty kilometres. Keepers polished the brass and wound the clockwork that spun the light through the night. Mordants like alum bind the colour to the fibre so it survives washing, and the same dye bath can give a dozen shades depending on the metal in the pot and the mineral content of the water.

As they retreat they leave behind moraines, long ridges of rubble that mark where the ice once ended, and meltwater lakes that glow a milky turquoise from the rock flour suspended in them. A fresh oil bites at the back of the throat, a peppery sting from antioxidants that fades within a year, which is why the harvest date matters far more than any label promising extra virgin purity. John Coltrane practised scales until his lips bled, chasing a sound that felt like prayer. His quartet turned a Broadway show tune into a spiritual suite that stretched past thirty minutes. Acclimatisation means climbing high and sleeping low, letting the blood thicken with new red cells over days, because rushing the mountain invites a fluid-filled swelling of the lungs or brain that kills quietly in the night.
